---
title: "Ingesting lab data"
subtitle: "The main focus of this testing round"
---

The central goal of this pilot is **getting cyanotoxin lab chemistry into FHAB and connected to
the right event / report / case**. Lab results arrive in several shapes — an email folder from
Bend Genetics, a CEDEN export, a one-off spreadsheet for a single site — so there are a few ways
in. This page walks each one, then the **reconciliation** step that links the data to events.

::: {.callout-tip title="Where these live"}
Everything below is under the top-level **Ingest Data** menu (staff only), except attaching
results to a single report/case, which is on the report/case page itself.
:::

## Pick the path that matches how the data arrived

| The lab data arrived as… | Use | Menu |
|---|---|---|
| A **folder of email attachments** from Bend/partner (a results spreadsheet + chain-of-custody / transmittal / receipt PDFs) | **Ingest lab email folders** | Ingest Data → *Ingest lab email folders* |
| A **CEDEN WaterChemistry CSV** (the Bend→CEDEN workflow output) | **Upload CEDEN lab data** | Ingest Data → *Upload CEDEN lab data* |
| A **full CEDEN chemistry template** where StationCode + date is the only link to an event | **Lab batch reconciliation** | Ingest Data → *Lab batch reconciliation* |
| Results for **one already-known report or case** | **Attach on the report/case** | Reports / Cases → *Lab upload* |

All paths land the samples where you can **reconcile** them (link to events) on the **Lab data
workboard**. Start with the workflow that matches your file; the rest of the page is the same
downstream.

---

## A. Email folder upload (Bend / partner) — the newest path

Labs typically email a **folder**: a wide-format results spreadsheet (analytes across columns)
plus a scanned **chain-of-custody (CoC)**, a **transmittal letter**, and a **sample-receipt
form**. This path converts that spreadsheet, brings the samples in, and **keeps the original
files on the batch** for provenance.

**Steps**

1. **Ingest Data → Ingest lab email folders**.
2. Under *Folder files*, select **all** the files from the one email folder (the results CSV/XLSX
   **and** the PDFs). Add a **Source label** like `Clear Lake (RB5)` (optional but helpful).
3. **Ingest folder.** You'll get a summary — *"Ingested N samples (M geocoded), K results; stored
   J files"* — and land on the workboard filtered to that batch.

**What happens**

- The wide spreadsheet is converted to CEDEN long form: each analyte value becomes a result
  (ELISA toxins, dry-weight toxins, qPCR genes, chlorophyll). `ND` becomes a proper non-detect.
- Samples come in **unlinked** and are **geocoded from the CEDEN station registry** when the
  station code is known. Samples whose station isn't in the registry come in **ungeocoded** —
  their coordinates usually live on the CoC (see [Geocoding](#geocoding-ungeocoded-samples)).
- The **source files stay on the batch**: on the workboard batch banner (and the *Ingested
  batches* table) you'll see `coc · data · receipt · transmittal` links — they open in a **new
  tab** so your workspace stays put.

**What to test**

- Upload a folder; do the sample/result/geocoded counts look right?
- Open the stored **CoC** link — does it open in a new tab and show the scanned form?
- A folder with **no results spreadsheet** (only PDFs) should still store the files.
- Do the samples appear on the **workboard**, filtered to the new batch?

---

## B. Upload CEDEN lab data (workflow output)

For a **CEDEN WaterChemistry CSV** (the output of the Bend→CEDEN workflow), use **Ingest Data →
Upload CEDEN lab data**. You can upload a file or pull from a URL. This loads samples + results,
geocodes stations from the registry, and **auto-links** samples to nearby events where it's
confident.

**What to test:** upload a CEDEN chemistry CSV; confirm samples/results load and that obvious
station+date matches auto-link (the rest wait on the workboard).

---

## C. Lab batch reconciliation (CEDEN template, staged)

For a **full CEDEN chemistry template** where you want to review every link before it's written,
use **Ingest Data → Lab batch reconciliation**.

1. Upload the template CSV. Optionally widen the **match radius (m)** and **± days** window.
2. The batch is **staged** — nothing touches live reports yet.
3. For each **station + date group**, the matcher suggests candidate reports within the windows.
   **Promote** a confident match, **link** manually, or **create a report** from the station.
4. Use **Auto-link** to accept all high-confidence, unambiguous matches at once, then review the
   rest.

**What to test:** stage a template; confirm nothing is linked until you act; try Auto-link, then
link one group manually and create a report from another.

---

## D. Attach results to one report or case

When results belong to a **single known site**, skip matching entirely: open the **report** (or
**case**) and use its **Lab upload** to attach a CEDEN CSV directly. Every sample pins to that
report/case.

---

## After ingest: reconcile on the Lab data workboard

**Ingest Data → Lab data workboard** is where lab samples get connected to events/reports/cases
and quality-reviewed. It's built as a **task board**: the lead **assigns** work, team members
**link**, a reviewer **QA-approves** or **flags**.

- **Status chips:** *Unlinked*, *Linked · awaiting QA*, *Flagged for rework*, *QA approved*, and
  *Routine sampling* — click one to filter.
- **Filters:** status, assignee (incl. **Me** / Unassigned), region, and a station / water-body
  search. A batch link filters the board to just that ingest.
- **Assign:** check rows, pick a team member under *"With checked rows →"*, **Assign**.
- **Per sample:** **Link / Re-link** by report (R#) or case (C#); **+Report** (create one from the
  station); **✓ QA** approve; **⚑** flag with a reason; **Unlink**; **Routine** (see below).
  *Re-linking clears QA so the sample returns to the review queue.*
- **Batch reconcile:** *Reconcile checked* or *Reconcile all unlinked in this view* auto-links the
  confident, unambiguous matches in one pass.

### The sample map (🗺)

Click a sample's **🗺** to open its context map: the sample as a **★**, and nearby reports colored
by how close in **time** they are to the sample (blue ≤14 d, yellow 14–30 d, pink 30–60 d, gray
>60 d). Click a nearby marker to **link to that report or its case**, or open the report (new
tab).

### Geocoding ungeocoded samples {#geocoding-ungeocoded-samples}

Samples whose station isn't in the registry come in **without a location** — their coordinates
are usually on the **CoC**. On the sample map you can:

1. Open the batch's **CoC** (new tab) and read the latitude/longitude.
2. Type them into the **lat / long** boxes → **Search nearby** to preview reports/cases around
   that point (shown as a ◆).
3. **Save to sample** to store the coordinates — the sample is now geocoded, appears on the main
   map, and becomes eligible for batch reconcile.
4. **Scan CoC (OCR)** will try to read the coordinates off the CoC automatically. *In the current
   hosted pilot this is a best-effort helper and may report that OCR isn't available* — if so,
   just read the CoC and type the numbers.

::: {.callout-tip title="Many at once — Bulk sample coordinates"}
To geocode a whole batch quickly, use **Ingest Data → Bulk sample coordinates**: paste one row
per line as `station code, latitude, longitude` (station codes with spaces/commas are fine — the
last two numbers are taken as lat/long). Matching a station geocodes every sample at it.
:::

### Routine sampling (not every sample is a bloom report)

Some samples are **routine monitoring**, not tied to any bloom report or case. On an unlinked
sample, use **Routine** (row button or the map popup's *Tag routine sampling*) to move it into the
**Routine sampling** bucket so it stops showing as unfinished work. **↩ Unlinked** puts it back.
*(Routine sub-types will come later.)*

---

## Suggested lab-ingest test script (~15 min)

1. **Ingest an email folder** (Ingest Data → *Ingest lab email folders*) — pick all files from one
   lab folder, add a source label, ingest. Check the counts and open the stored **CoC** (new tab).
2. On the **workboard** (it opens filtered to your batch), find an **unlinked, geocoded** sample,
   open its **🗺**, and **link** it to a nearby report — or **+Report** to create one.
3. Find an **ungeocoded** sample; read its coordinates off the CoC, type them in, **Search
   nearby**, then **Save to sample**.
4. Try **Bulk sample coordinates**: paste two or three `station, lat, lon` rows and apply.
5. Tag one sample as **Routine sampling**, then confirm it appears under the *Routine* chip.
6. Run **Reconcile all unlinked in this view** and see how many auto-link.
7. Open **Lab** (results browser) and confirm your batch's results appear; **Download CSV**.
8. Note anything confusing along the way — see [Giving feedback](feedback.qmd).
